meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Master advanced project management to overcome debugging roadblocks. A high-level checklist for students using Antigravity and Claude Code for success.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a code issue; it's a structural failure. By implementing rigorous project management, you isolate variables, document state transitions, and create reproducible environments. Use Claude Code to automate trace analysis and Antigravity to visualize architectural bottlenecks, turning chaotic bug-hunting into a systematic, repeatable engineering process.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, the cursor blinking in mocking rhythm. The stack trace is a labyrinth of red text, and every fix seems to spawn three new errors. You aren't failing as a developer; you are hitting the wall of unmanaged complexity that every senior engineer has faced. 

This isn't about knowing more syntax; it's about shifting from 'coding' to 'engineering.' By treating your codebase like a high-stakes project rather than a hobbyist experiment, you can demystify the most daunting bugs. We are going to deploy a professional project management framework to regain control of your logic.

steps.title: Define the Failure Boundary
steps.description: Before fixing, isolate the exact scope of the failure. Map your state transitions to identify where the data mutation deviates from the expected schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this module's state transition diagram and identify the point where the data schema breaks during asynchronous execution.

steps.title: Automate Trace Regression
steps.description: Create a sandbox environment to replicate the error consistently. If you can't reproduce it, you can't fix it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal reproduction script for the identified bug based on the provided stack trace.

steps.title: Implement Defensive Architecture
steps.description: Introduce guardrails that catch silent failures before they propagate into deep, unreadable errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function to implement strict type checking and error handling that provides verbose logs on failure.

steps.title: Audit Dependency Chains
steps.description: Debugging often hides in third-party library conflicts. Audit your dependency tree for version mismatch or deprecated calls.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current dependency tree and flag any packages causing compatibility issues with our current runtime.

steps.title: Systematic Change Tracking
steps.description: Use atomic commits and project logs to maintain a 'known-good' state. If a change fails, you must be able to roll back instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the last 5 changes and assess which one introduced the regression in the auth module.

pro_tips: Treat every bug as an opportunity to write a new unit test; never fix a bug without a test that proves the fix.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude Code: explain your logic step-by-step; the AI will often spot your logical fallacy before you do.
Adopt the '15-minute rule': if you haven't made progress in 15 minutes, step back and re-map the architecture instead of brute-forcing code.
Keep a 'Bug Journal' to track recurring patterns; you will notice your own bad habits manifest in specific parts of your stack.
Always check your environment variables first; 90% of 'impossible' bugs are just missing API keys or wrong path configurations.
content.next_steps: After completing this checklist, perform a post-mortem on your project. Document the root cause of your bugs and update your project README with a 'Lessons Learned' section.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to dismantle even the most complex bugs. Don't let your current project sit stagnant while you struggle with errors. Take the leap, apply these systems, and build something that lasts. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
